title: Storage, Filesystems, Archives, and Backups · Command Workshop
summary: Respond to low disk space and protect service data without confusing filesystems, mounts, archives, snapshots, and verified backups. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a storage map and restore-tested backup plan while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Relate block devices, partitions, filesystems, mount points, namespaces, and directory visibility.
Distinguish apparent size, allocated blocks, free space, inodes, deleted-open files, and per-directory usage.
Use lsblk, findmnt, mount, and filesystem identifiers to observe topology before any mount or format action.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
workshop case — Filesystem / is 92% full with 600 free inodes while a deleted-open log consumes 1.4 GiB. Build an archive and restore proof without formatting, mounting, or deleting host data.

What must be established before implementation?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for relate block devices, partitions, filesystems, mount points, namespaces, and directory visibility.

content.text: Filesystem / is 92% full with 600 free inodes while a deleted-open log consumes 1.4 GiB. Build an archive and restore proof without formatting, mounting, or deleting host data.

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Block Filesystem Mount condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: GNU Coreutils Manual — Controls file, text, identity, process, filesystem, and common utility behavior.

hints: Name the Block Filesystem Mount precondition or invariant that the workshop case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Filesystem / is 92% full with 600 free inodes while a deleted-open log consumes 1.4 GiB. Build an archive and restore proof without formatting, mounting, or deleting host data.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: A mounted device becomes a new drive letter independent of the directory tree.

options.text: Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

A mounted device becomes a new drive letter independent of the directory tree.
